title: Shinji and the Pendant Fun in Tang Land
display_title: Shinji and the Pendant Fun in Tang Land
wikitext:
[]: Shinji & the Pendant / Fun in Tang Land             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Mar 22, 2007 6:56 pm LOL MESSY SPLIT. Since the Technology around here blows, I need to put some of the content from posts I couldn't split from the original thread into other people's posts to maintain the proper sequence. (This stuff will be green.) My apologies. --Reichu ----------------------------- Reichu Thu Mar 22, 2007 6:53 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Anyway, another thought just occurred to me. That's Misato's cross on that pole. And you know what that means?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Mar 22, 2007 8:10 pm Ornette wrote: uhhh.. Shinji put it there?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 22, 2007 8:18 pm You could have just said so. Never noticed Shinji wearing the pendant during "Void in the Dream". Cute. ----------------------------- Anonymous_Evafan Thu Mar 22, 2007 8:38 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I have yet to hear a convincing explanation that the beach they find themselves on is even in Japan,

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 22, 2007 9:25 pm Reichu wrote: You could have just said so. Never noticed Shinji wearing the pendant during "Void in the Dream". Cute.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Mar 22, 2007 11:24 pm When the souls get released they scattered all over the earth, and it's my personal assumptions that they went back to the general proximity of where the person was before they got tanged. Shinji and Asuka were at the geofront during tangification, and since all that's left of the geofront is a big puddle of goo, they appeared near the shore of it. Simple enough for me!

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Thu Mar 22, 2007 11:54 pm Very cool to know that Shinji had the cross with him all that time. Just reaffirms how much he cared for Misato

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Mar 23, 2007 3:54 am Hmm, I never put Misato's cross and that sequence with Shinji together in my mind. I need to see EoE, again! X~l

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 24, 2007 7:21 pm To finally elaborate on my previous outburst.... The presence of Misato's cross gives us a very definite physical source of continutity betwen the images of Shinji's "journey" through episode #26'. Specifically, it's an anchor that tells us that Shinji was in fact within the entry plug the entire time. From the moment he entered GNR, amid the entire alternate universe dreamscape, while speaking with Rei and Kaworu, and when the left Unit-01 and descended in the plug down to earth somehow. After Reichu had finally drilled into my head what the signifgance of the "Void the Dream" sequence was, I brought up a point a while back , about Shinji being in an "interstice" in End of Evangelion, while speaking with Rei and Kaworu, and saying goodbye to his mother. ANF Thread:OMF wrote: This place where Shinji speaks with Rei, Yui and even Kaworu, is some kind of place between worlds, an interstice, neither part of complementation, or a part of the outside world. It's a private spot where he converses alone with the three Deities. I suppose it's a little like the ferry in the middle of the river Styx, neither part of the overworld or the underworld, but somewhere inbetween.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Mar 24, 2007 7:27 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Moreover, he is able to escape full complementation and retain his individuality, because the entry plug is protecting him somehow from the effects of Lilith/GNRs massive Anti AT Field, just as Misato's "plug like" escape pod protected her from Adam's AATF in during second impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Mar 24, 2007 7:45 pm Note the black and white orbs (supposedly moons/eggs) on either side of Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 24, 2007 8:00 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Protecting him how? He went sploosh just like everyone else during 3I, Misato did not do the same during 2I. There is no connection here.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 24, 2007 8:29 pm Szmitten wrote: Note the black and white orbs (supposedly moons/eggs) on either side of Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Mar 24, 2007 10:15 pm Reichu wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji's tangification in in #26' is more likely to be akin to his one in episode #19, rather than the forced AATF induced tangifications in EoE. In others words, the mechanisms behind each are different.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 24, 2007 11:27 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: And don't forget Shinji, as stated by Rei, was ONE with EVERYTHING else. If the plug was protecting him I don't see how that would be possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Mar 25, 2007 2:15 pm Why would she be BSing about this? I said it before, just chalk it up to her bizzaro Quantum Magic powers.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 25, 2007 4:21 pm Shinji is most likely not as one with everything else when talking to Rei/Lilith, the personage who has just absorbed all souls. He is still an individual, but is perilously close to absolute union with Rei/Lilith, as perhaps is symbolised by their physical and extraordinary union in this scene. He is still an individual because the entry plug is keeping him that way. I think it's time to discuss what would have happened if Shinji had indeed chosen complementation. With the plug in the way, and himself tangified, it's unlikely he could pull any hatches open. Instead I think what would have happened is that Shinji would have merged with Unit-01, as had almost happened in #20, and then Shinji/Yui in Unit-01, the ex last two individuals would have joined with Lilith in the one complemented being. At least, that's one idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Mar 25, 2007 4:47 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji is most likely not as one with everything else when talking to Rei/Lilith, the personage who has just absorbed all souls. He is still an individual, but is perilously close to absolute union with Rei/Lilith, as perhaps is symbolised by their physical and extraordinary union in this scene. He is still an individual because the entry plug is keeping him that way.

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Sun Mar 25, 2007 11:48 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji is most likely not as one with everything else when talking to Rei/Lilith, the personage who has just absorbed all souls. He is still an individual, but is perilously close to absolute union with Rei/Lilith, as perhaps is symbolised by their physical and extraordinary union in this scene. He is still an individual because the entry plug is keeping him that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Mar 25, 2007 11:56 pm ORAMI!? wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Shinji is most likely not as one with everything else when talking to Rei/Lilith, the personage who has just absorbed all souls. He is still an individual, but is perilously close to absolute union with Rei/Lilith, as perhaps is symbolised by their physical and extraordinary union in this scene. He is still an individual because the entry plug is keeping him that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 25, 2007 11:56 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: He is still an individual because the entry plug is keeping him that way. I think it's time to discuss... <snip>
